channel.title: Notes on stultus notes
channel.description: Recent content in Notes on stultus notes

item.title: Cardinal-Direction Languages Anchor Cognition to Landscape
item.description: Speakers of languages that use north/south/east/west instead of left/right stay continuously oriented and arrange time relative to the landscape rather than the body.

item.title: Colour Vocabulary Sharpens Perceptual Discrimination
item.description: Russian distinguishes light blue (goluboy) from dark blue (siniy) lexically. Russian speakers discriminate the two faster, and their brains show a categorical-shift response that English speakers’ brains do not.

item.title: Event Framing Shapes Memory and Blame
item.description: How a sentence frames an event — agentive ‘he broke the vase’ vs non-agentive ’the vase broke’ — changes what witnesses remember and how much blame they assign, even when they watched the same video.

item.title: How Language Shapes the Way We Think — Lera Boroditsky (TED)
item.description: Cognitive scientist Lera Boroditsky surveys lab evidence that the structure of a language shapes how its speakers think — about space, time, number, colour, causation, and blame.

item.title: Language Shapes the Way We Think
item.description: The structure of a language — its vocabulary, grammar, and obligatory distinctions — shapes how its speakers attend, remember, and reason. Linguistic relativity, with lab evidence.

item.title: Number Words Are a Cognitive Stepping-Stone
item.description: Exact counting is a linguistic trick — name each item with a number, the last name is the count. Without number words, exact-quantity tasks become unsolvable, and the entire downstream world of mathematics is closed off.

item.title: A Thinking Falling Stone Would Believe It Chose to Fall
item.description: Spinoza’s analogy: a stone falling under gravity, if granted consciousness, would interpret its fall as a desire — confusing necessity for choice.

item.title: Free Will Is an Illusion (Spinoza)
item.description: Spinoza’s claim that what we feel as free choice is determined by prior causes — neurons, history, disposition — and merely experienced as freedom.

item.title: Political Freedom Can Coexist With Determinism
item.description: Einstein admired Spinoza’s denial of free will yet fought for democracy and human rights — the two commitments operate at different levels and need not contradict.

item.title: Spinoza, Einstein, and the Illusion of Free Will (Avshalom Elitzur)
item.description: Avshalom Elitzur on Spinoza’s denial of free will, Einstein’s love for him, and the paradox of believing in political freedom while doubting metaphysical free will.

item.title: EARS: Easy Approach to Requirements Syntax
item.description: EARS is a template-based method for writing natural language requirements that are clear, testable, and unambiguous. Five patterns — ubiquitous, event-driven, unwanted behavior, state-driven, and optional — cover most real-world needs.

item.title: ActivityPub is the W3C protocol that powers the fediverse
item.description: ActivityPub is a W3C standard that defines how federated servers communicate using actors, inboxes, and a shared activity vocabulary.

item.title: Circuit breaker pattern stops cascading failures in distributed systems
item.description: The circuit breaker pattern prevents a system from wasting resources on a failing remote dependency by short-circuiting requests after consecutive failures.

item.title: Federated search distributes queries across independent indexes
item.description: Federated search solves the problem of finding the best results across N independent search indexes without querying all of them.

item.title: Federation is many independent servers speaking a shared protocol
item.description: Federation applies the email model to any networked application — independent servers interoperate through a shared protocol without central coordination.

item.title: HTTP Signatures prove identity in federation without shared secrets
item.description: HTTP Signatures use public-key cryptography to verify that a federated message actually came from the server it claims to be from.

item.title: Reciprocal Rank Fusion merges ranked lists without comparable scores
item.description: RRF combines ranked result lists from multiple sources using only rank positions, avoiding the problem of incomparable scoring systems.

item.title: Relay pattern reduces N-squared delivery to 2N in federation
item.description: A relay instance acts as a hub that redistributes activities, reducing the delivery cost from N×(N-1) direct connections to 2N.

item.title: WebFinger resolves handles to identity documents in federation
item.description: WebFinger (RFC 7033) is a simple discovery protocol that maps a handle like @user@domain to the URL of an actor’s identity document.

item.title: Chatha Pacha: The Ring of Rowdies
Jerry Rigging
item.description: A blend of ‘jury-rigged’ and ‘jerry-built’ meaning to make a makeshift repair or construct something quickly using only materials at hand.

item.title: Jugaad
item.description: A Hindi term for a flexible, innovative, and resourceful approach to problem-solving that emphasizes using limited resources to create quick fixes or frugal innovations.

item.title: Kludge
item.description: A workaround or makeshift solution that is clumsy, inelegant, and hard to maintain, but rapidly solves an important problem using available resources.

item.title: Chinese Settlement in Calicut
item.description: Thousands of Chinese soldiers lived in Calicut during the Ming dynasty, leaving behind a mixed Malayali-Chinese community called Chinna Kribala.

item.title: Legend of Prester John
item.description: A mythical Christian priest-king whom medieval Europeans believed ruled a fabulously rich and powerful Christian kingdom somewhere ‘in the East’.

item.title: The Ivory Throne by Manu S Pillai
item.description: A historical account of the Travancore royal family and the life of Sethu Lakshmi Bayi, the last Maharani Regent of Travancore.

item.title: 6 Archetypes of Broken Ownership
item.description: Explores six archetypes of broken ownership when knowledge, mandate, or responsibility are missing, and how to fix them.

item.title: Broken Ownership Archetypes
item.description: Six patterns of broken ownership that occur when Knowledge, Mandate, or Responsibility are missing.

item.title: Broken Windows Theory
item.description: Theory that visible signs of disorder encourage further disorder. In software, tech debt compounds as teams care less about quality over time.

item.title: Eisenhower Matrix
item.description: A prioritization framework that categorizes tasks by urgency and importance into four quadrants.

item.title: Friends
item.description: Digital gardens, blogs, and websites of friends.

item.title: Goals-Signals-Metrics (GSM) Model
item.description: A three-step methodology for translating abstract objectives into measurable outcomes: Goals → Signals → Metrics.

item.title: HEART Framework
item.description: A user-centered methodology that measures UX quality through five key metrics: Happiness, Engagement, Adoption, Retention, and Task Success.

item.title: Invictus
item.description: A poem about unconquerable spirit and mastery of one’s fate.

item.title: Net Promoter Score (NPS)
item.description: A customer loyalty metric measuring how likely customers are to recommend a product or service, calculated as % Promoters - % Detractors.

item.title: Ownership Trio
item.description: True ownership requires three interdependent components: Knowledge, Mandate, and Responsibility.

item.title: Podcast Etymology
item.description: The word ‘podcast’ was coined by Ben Hammersley in February 2004 as a blend of ‘iPod’ and ‘broadcast’.

item.title: RICE Framework
item.description: A prioritization framework for evaluating features and projects using Reach, Impact, Confidence, and Effort.

item.title: You Build It, You Own It
item.description: Philosophy that teams should fully own what they build, not just run it—encompassing knowledge, mandate, and responsibility.

item.title: Adaptability
item.description: The ability to adjust to new conditions and circumstances.

item.title: Amateur
item.description: The enthusiast who pursues her work in the spirit of love.

item.title: Amateur Mindset
item.description: Embracing the beginner’s mind and incremental improvement over perfectionism.

item.title: Analytical Data
item.description: Data that helps humans make better decisions.

item.title: Analytically Operational Data
item.description: Data that automatically helps someone make better decisions.

item.title: Architecturally-significant Requirement (ASR)
item.description: A requirement that measurably influences a software system’s architecture.

item.title: Architecture Decision (AD)
item.description: A software design choice that addresses an architecturally-significant requirement.

item.title: Architecture Decision Log (ADL)
item.description: A collection of all Architecture Decision Records for a project or organization.

item.title: Avoid Stupidity
item.description: Avoiding stupidity is easier than trying to be brilliant. Focus on eliminating failure points.

item.title: Boundary Setting
item.description: The practice of defining and communicating limits to protect your values, time, and well-being.

item.title: Creativity is a Way of Operating - John Cleese
item.description: Creativity is not a talent, it is a way of operating.

item.title: Deductive Reasoning - Eliminate the Impossible
item.description: When you have eliminated the impossible, whatever remains, however improbable, must be the truth.

item.title: Design of Experiments (DoE)
item.description: A formal way to design a task that explains the variation under conditions.

item.title: Dunder in Python
item.description: Double underscore methods and attributes in Python that provide special functionality to classes and objects.

item.title: Dunning-Kruger Effect
item.description: Cognitive bias where people with low ability overestimate their competence, while high performers may underestimate theirs.

item.title: ELT (Extract - Load - Transform)
item.description: Data processing approach where raw data is loaded before transformation, preserving original data.

item.title: Find Your Voice - Dan Harmon
item.description: Find your voice, shout it from the rooftops, and keep doing it until the people that are looking for you find you.

item.title: Genomic Sequencing Service
item.description: NHS service that sequences your genome to early detect health conditions due to changes to a few of your genes.

item.title: Growth Mindset vs Fixed Mindset
item.description: Carol Dweck’s framework: believing abilities can be developed vs believing they’re fixed.

item.title: Highest Divorce Rate
item.description: Maldives reportedly has the highest divorce rate, despite being a supposedly religious country.

item.title: Hope Is a Good Thing
item.description: Hope is perhaps the best of things, and no good thing ever dies.

item.title: Human Brain Memory Capacity
item.description: The memory capacity of the human brain is estimated to be around 2.5 petabytes.

item.title: Inversion
item.description: Looking at a problem or decision from the opposite point of view to avoid failure rather than achieve success.

item.title: Lone Genius Myth
item.description: The false narrative that great work emerges from isolated individuals with superhuman talents.

item.title: Making a Difference (The Star Thrower)
item.description: Making a difference to one is making a difference, regardless of scale.

item.title: Miranda Warning
item.description: A warning given to criminal suspects in the United States about their constitutional rights.

item.title: Mortality Awareness
item.description: Using awareness of death to clarify priorities and make meaningful choices.

item.title: Operational Data
item.description: Data whose purpose is to remember things.

item.title: Phantom Urbanism
item.description: Buildings built as financial instruments, unlived in, massive in scale.

item.title: Pre-Mortem Analysis
item.description: A technique to identify potential failures before they happen by imagining the project has already failed.

item.title: Pretty Good House
item.description: Ideas for building a pretty good house.

item.title: Primary Sources in Historical Research
item.description: The importance of primary sources - firsthand accounts from people who were actually there.

item.title: Process Documentation
item.description: Becoming a documentarian of your work to track progress and create shareable content.

item.title: Process is Messy
item.description: You have to make stuff. Share something small every day.

item.title: Resilience
item.description: The ability to recover from setbacks and adapt to adversity.

item.title: Retraction Watch
item.description: A website that lists papers retracted from scientific publishing due to mistakes or data manipulation.

item.title: Scenius
item.description: An ecology of talent where great ideas emerge from collaborative creative communities.

item.title: Show Your Work! by Austin Kleon
item.description: 10 ways to share your creativity and get discovered - a guide to showing your work and building an audience.

item.title: Silence and Unspoken Words
item.description: The tragedy of silence - when unspoken words become permanent regrets.

item.title: The Compass of Zettelkasten
item.description: A method for exploring ideas using four compass directions to understand an idea from different perspectives.

item.title: Theory of Constraints
item.description: A system is only as strong as its weakest point. Focus on the bottleneck and optimize the entire system.

item.title: Character (Story Element)
item.description: The protagonist’s role in evoking empathy and driving the story.

item.title: Conflict (Story Element)
item.description: Obstacles that generate emotional investment.

item.title: Desire (Story Element)
item.description: The driving force of the protagonist.

item.title: Digital Garden Workflow
item.description: How to maintain this digital garden using Zettelkasten principles.

item.title: Screenwriting Storytelling Blueprint
item.description: Notes from the course ‘Screenwriting & Storytelling Blueprint: Hero’s Two Journeys’.

item.title: Story
item.description: Story is a vehicle for emotional experience and transformation.

item.title: La France sous leurs yeux - 200 regards de photographes sur les années 2020
The School for Good and Evil
Rebel Moon – Part One: A Child of Fire
The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind
Kingdom: Return of the Great General
9-1-1 (TV Series)
item.description: Wisdom and insights from the 9-1-1 TV series on decision-making and resilience.

item.title: AI Adoption
item.description: The process of integrating AI tools and practices into an organization.

item.title: Amazon's 6-Pager
item.description: Amazon’s narrative-driven decision document format for meetings and proposals.

item.title: Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
item.description: Lightweight documentation format for recording architectural decisions and their context.

item.title: Bazel BUILD Style Guide
item.description: Official Bazel style guide recommending DAMP over DRY for BUILD files.

item.title: Bhargavee Nilayam Filming Locations - Investigation
item.description: Investigation debunking the myth that Bhargavee Nilayam was shot at Nilambur DFO Bungalow.

item.title: Coachable vs Learnable vs Intrinsic Skills
item.description: Framework for categorizing skills by how they can be developed.

item.title: Coachable, Learnable, and Intrinsic - Hiring Framework
item.description: Framework for understanding which skills are coachable, learnable, or intrinsic when hiring.

item.title: DAMP Principle
item.description: Descriptive and Meaningful Phrases - a principle encouraging readability over uniqueness.

item.title: Decision Document Framework by Arnav Kumar
item.description: Arnav Kumar’s framework for writing comprehensive decision documents to get faster approvals.

item.title: Do Not Stand at My Grave and Weep - Mary Elizabeth Frye
item.description: Comforting poem about transcending death and being present in nature.

item.title: DORA
item.description: DevOps Research and Assessment - research program studying software delivery performance.

item.title: DRY Principle
item.description: Don’t Repeat Yourself - a principle encouraging uniqueness through abstraction.

item.title: Eliminate the Impossible - Sherlock Holmes
item.description: Sherlock Holmes’s principle of deductive reasoning - eliminate the impossible.

item.title: Flexibility Over Rigidity
item.description: Resilience comes from flexibility, not rigidity - like redwoods that bend with the wind.

item.title: Google Maps
item.description: Google’s mapping and navigation service that uses crowdsourced data for traffic information.

item.title: Handle Me at My Worst - Marilyn Monroe
item.description: Quote about accepting someone’s flaws along with their strengths.

item.title: Hope Is a Good Thing - Shawshank Redemption
item.description: Quote about hope being the best of things that never dies.

item.title: Imperfection Is Beauty - Marilyn Monroe
item.description: Quote about embracing imperfection, madness, and being ridiculous over boring.

item.title: Ingress
item.description: Augmented reality mobile game by Niantic that may have been used to improve Google Maps data.

item.title: Internal Platform
item.description: Infrastructure and tooling built for internal developer use within an organization.

item.title: Intrinsic Qualities in Hiring
item.description: Why intrinsic qualities matter most in hiring and cannot be easily developed.

item.title: Listen to Your Instincts - Judith McNaught
item.description: Quote about following your instincts when they defy logic.

item.title: One OS to Rule Them All - Linux Parody
item.description: Parody of Tolkien’s Ring Verse celebrating Linux and open source.

item.title: Passion Not Spent - Caleb Madrigal
item.description: Poem about the fear of dying with passion unspent and not truly living.

item.title: Petition Against Ayyankali - 1914
item.description: Historical petition from 1914 showing resistance faced by social reformer Ayyankali.

item.title: Phantom Media
item.description: Media property devoid of news, designed as investment vehicle rather than content.

item.title: Platform Engineering
item.description: Building internal platforms as products to improve developer experience and productivity.

item.title: RACI Matrix
item.description: Responsibility assignment framework defining who is Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, and Informed.

item.title: Request for Comments (RFC)
item.description: Collaborative decision-making process where proposals are shared for feedback before finalization.

item.title: Right No vs Wrong Yes
item.description: Sometimes the right kind of No is better than the wrong kind of Yes.

item.title: Silence and Unspoken Words - Anshuman Dash
item.description: Quote about unspoken words and the gap between what we feel and what we say.

item.title: Some Birds Aren't Meant to Be Caged - Stephen King
item.description: Quote about birds too bright to be caged and missing friends who fly away.

item.title: State of AI-assisted Software Development 2025 (DORA Report)
item.description: DORA’s 2025 research on AI’s impact on software development, revealing AI as an amplifier of organizational strengths and weaknesses.

item.title: Tell The People You Love That You Love Them
item.description: Essay about being straightforward with love and not pretending not to care.

item.title: The Road Goes Ever On - J.R.R. Tolkien
item.description: Tolkien’s poem about the endless journey through life, appearing in The Hobbit and The Lord of the Rings.

item.title: The Sky Is Not the Limit - Sawyer Rosenstein
item.description: Quote about limitless possibilities when there are footprints on the moon.

item.title: The Star Thrower (Starfish Story)
item.description: Story about making a difference one starfish at a time, despite overwhelming odds.

item.title: Value Stream Mapping
item.description: A lean management technique for visualizing and analyzing the flow of work through a system.

item.title: We're All Stories in the End - Doctor Who
item.description: The Eleventh Doctor’s farewell about being stories in the end.

item.title: You Are the Reason - Unknown
item.description: Quote about how love brings light to sadness, understanding to confusion, and trust to betrayal.

item.title: സ്മാരകം (Smarakam) - Veerankutty
item.description: Malayalam poem about the humble flight of a dandelion seed and the monument it becomes.

item.title: The Boy, the Mole, the Fox and the Horse
Sniper: G.R.I.T. - Global Response & Intelligence Team
Engineering Strategy
item.description: How to write good engineering strategies

item.title: Wardley Maps
item.description: A strategy visualization tool.

item.title: Minimum Viable Product (MVP)
item.description: A version of a product with just enough features to be usable by early customers.

item.title: Proof of Concept (POC)
item.description: A realization of a certain method or idea to demonstrate its feasibility.

item.title: Prototype
item.description: An early sample or model of a product built to test a concept.

item.title: Polymath
item.description: A person who knows a lot about different subjects.

item.title: Plaintiff
item.description: Legal definition of a party bringing a suit.

item.title: Local vs Distributed Objects
item.description: The fundamental differences between local and distributed object interactions.

item.title: Paper: A Note On Distributed Computing
item.description: A critique of the ‘unified view’ of local and distributed objects.

item.title: A Note on Distributed Computing
item.description: The thesis that local and distributed objects are fundamentally different.

item.title: Application Layout Engine
item.description: Systems managing the arrangement of UI components across different devices and screen sizes.

item.title: Document Layout Engine
item.description: Software for rendering and formatting document layouts.

item.title: Layout Engine
item.description: Software component that displays formatted content.

item.title: User Interface Markup Language
item.description: Markup languages for defining user interfaces.

item.title: Widget Toolkit
item.description: A library for building graphical user interfaces.

item.title: Footnotes in Markdown
item.description: Syntax to create footnotes in Markdown

item.title: Influencer Marketing
item.description: Strategies and data regarding marketing through influential individuals.

item.title: Digital Garden
item.description: A personal tool for thinking and writing with hypertextual features.

item.title: HBR: When It Comes to Influencers, Smaller Can Be Better
item.description: Research on the ROI of nano vs macro influencers.

item.title: Influencer Marketing Funnel
item.description: Four tiers of influencer marketing measurement.

item.title: Nano Influencers vs Macro Influencers
item.description: Comparison of ROI between small and large influencers.

item.title: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 1
Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 2
Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince
Rurouni Kenshin Part III: The Legend Ends
Brave New Words: How AI Will Revolutionize Education (and Why That's a Good Thing)
The Librarian of Basra: A True Story from Iraq
Kingdom III: The Flame of Destiny
Curry & Cyanide: The Jolly Joseph Case
The Goal: A Process of Ongoing Improvement
Clear Thinking: Turning Ordinary Moments into Extraordinary Results
stultus
item.description: stultus is the online pseudonym of Hrishikesh Bhaskaran. A Latin word meaning stupid or foolish, adopted as a handle across the internet.

item.title: Cyber Bullying
item.description: The use of electronic communication to bully a person, typically by sending messages of an intimidating or threatening nature.

item.title: Cyber Crime
item.description: Overview of cyber crime, online violence, and legal protections in India.

item.title: Constitution of India - Article 21
item.description: Protection of life and personal liberty.

item.title: Cyber Bullying Laws in India
item.description: Legal provisions covering cyberbullying in India.

item.title: Cyber Bullying Laws International
item.description: Overview of cyberbullying laws in different countries.

item.title: Cyber Crime Laws in India
item.description: Key legal provisions protecting against online abuse in India.

item.title: Doxxing
item.description: Revealing personal details online without consent.

item.title: Online Violence
item.description: Forms of harassment and abuse faced on the internet.

item.title: Turn the Ship Around!: A True Story of Turning Followers into Leaders
Rocky Aur Rani Kii Prem Kahaani
The Pirates: The Last Royal Treasure
Blink: The Power of Thinking Without Thinking
Accelerate: Building and Scaling High Performing Technology Organizations
Never Split the Difference: Negotiating As If Your Life Depended On It
Bad Blood: Secrets and Lies in a Silicon Valley Startup
Build: An Unorthodox Guide to Making Things Worth Making

Hello World: Being Human in the Age of Algorithms
Escaping the Build Trap: How Effective Product Management Creates Real Value

The Mom Test: How to talk to customers & learn if your business is a good idea when everyone is lying to you
The Phoenix Project: A Novel About IT, DevOps, and Helping Your Business Win
Till the Clouds Roll By: Beginning Again
Talking to My Daughter: A Brief History of Capitalism
Down and Dirty Pictures: Miramax, Sundance, and the Rise of Independent Film
Screenwriting 101: Mastering the Art of Story
Save the Cat: The Last Book on Screenwriting You'll Ever Need
Grit: The Power of Passion and Perseverance
Surya: How the Sun God was Tamed
Peak: Secrets From The New Science of Expertise
Practice Perfect: 42 Rules for Getting Better at Getting Better
Super Thinking: The Big Book of Mental Models
Indistractable: How to Control Your Attention and Choose Your Life
Bookworm: A Memoir of Childhood Reading
Algorithms to Live By: The Computer Science of Human Decisions
Start with Why: How Great Leaders Inspire Everyone to Take Action
No Place to Hide: Edward Snowden, the NSA, and the U.S. Surveillance State
Emotional Intelligence: Why It Can Matter More Than IQ
Atomic Habits: An Easy & Proven Way to Build Good Habits & Break Bad Ones
Sleep Smarter: 21 Essential Strategies to Sleep Your Way to A Better Body, Better Health, and Bigger Success
The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People: Powerful Lessons in Personal Change
The Tao of Pooh and the Te of Piglet

Murder on the Orient Express (Hercule Poirot, #10)
The Cathedral & the Bazaar: Musings on Linux and Open Source by an Accidental Revolutionary
Free as in Freedom: Richard Stallman's Crusade for Free Software
Palathum parayum pathirum | പലതും പറയും പതിരും
Malayalasahithya Charithram Muthirnna Kuttikalkk | മലയാള സാഹിത്യചരിത്ര്യം മുതിർന്ന കുട്ടികൾക്ക്

Rise of the Planet of the Apes
Dawn of the Planet of the Apes
Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them
Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
അപഥ സഞ്ചാരികള്‍ക്ക് ഒരു കൈപ്പുസ്തകം | Apadha Sanjarikalkk Oru Kaippusthakam
പുസ്തകങ്ങളെ അതിരുവിട്ടു സ്നേഹിച്ചവന്‍ : ഒരു പുസ്തകക്കള്ളന്റെയും അയാളെ പിന്തുടര്‍ന്ന പുസ്തകപ്രേമിയുടേയും കഥ
Alice’s Adventures in Wonderland / Through the Looking-Glass
Mukesh Babu & party in Dubai | മുകേഷ് ബാബു & പാര്‍ട്ടി ഇന്‍ ദുബായ്

മുകേഷ് കഥകള്‍ ജീവിതത്തിലെ നേരും നര്‍മ്മവും | Mukesh Kathakal - Jeevithathile Nerum Narmmavum
മുരളി ഗോപിയുടെ കഥകൾ | Murali Gopiyude Kadhakal
മീസാന്‍ കല്ലുകളുടെ കാവല്‍ | Meesan Kallukalute Kaval
Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
Zen Pencils: Dream the Impossible Dream - Volume Two
Ee Stationil Ottaykk | ഈ സ്റ്റേഷനില്‍ ഒറ്റയ്ക്ക്
മഞ്ഞ മരങ്ങള്‍ ചുറ്റിലും | Manja marangal chuttilum
The Tale of The Princess Kaguya
Zen Pencils: Cartoon Quotes from Inspirational Folks (Volume 1)
പെണ്‍പഞ്ചതന്ത്രം, മറ്റു കഥകളും | Pen Panchathanthram, Mattu Kathakalum
Manjukalam Notta Kuthira | മഞ്ഞുകാലം നോറ്റ കുതിര
ഒരു ഹിജഡയുടെ ആത്മകഥ | Oru Hijadayude aathmakadha
